lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: Greetings, traveller.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's with all these Oathbreakers?

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: Gah! A bunch of fanatical cultists. Nothing more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't sound impressed?

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: No! Well I wouldn't be, would I? I've spent my whole life dedicated to my sect. I dreamed of one day entering the Sanctuary and finally meeting Mother!

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: Yet instead I'm greeted by this condescending 'Lord' who feels I could try being 'a little more chaotic' for once.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: And to top it all off, Mother hasn't awoken at all. I fear she is probably being held captive by this Nightmare, or worse.

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: The only things these oafs are breaking is my head with their silent stares and furious hand-talk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry your day hasn't been going well.

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: Ah... Thank you, traveller. Are you here to fight the Nightmare?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll see. Sounds pretty tough.

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: I'll pray for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They seem to be carrying dangerous weapons.

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: Indeed. Speaking of weapons, I assume you have heard of the immensely powerful orbs the Nightmare is protecting?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I have. In fact, I've managed to beat the Nightmare myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyway, as you've heard of the orbs I assume you've also heard of the even stronger Bashyhammer of Ashihama?

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: The what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Smashyhammer of Ashihama.

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: But you just said...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...Trashyhammer of Ashihama. Yes, I know what I said.

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: Wha-

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Slashyhammer?

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: That doesn't even make sense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dashyhammer? Mashyhammer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Flashyhammer?

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: Too far. I'm done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's with the mask?

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: These are the masks of the Sisterhood. While symbolic, they also have practical uses.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really? What like?

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: Herbs can be stored within the beak, masking the smell of death and preventing us from becoming infected.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds handy. Can I have one?

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: You ask too many questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that a yes?

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: It's a no. To wear a mask of the Sisterhood, you need to join the Sisterhood.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is the Sisterhood?

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: An ancient order, born right here in Slepe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well sign me up!

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: You lack the qualities we're currently looking for. Perhaps another time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the Sisterhood.

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: Many years ago, the Sisterhood were a group of healers dedicated to Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: However, our leader, a justiciar known as Phosani, saw the truth in Saradomin's teachings and turned away from his false light.

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: Phosani embraced the glory of Zamorak and most of our order did the same. After that, she became known as the Inquisitor, though our order refers to her as 'Mother'.

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: After the order reached enlightenment through Zamorak, we made our home in an abandoned hermitage. Over time, that hermitage grew into the town you see today.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to those that refused to turn away from Saradomin?

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: They were shown the error of their ways through fire and blood.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How wonderful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Sister Aseret
lines.text: Be safe, traveller.
